definition: receive, agree to
I accept your apology.
to perform an action
He decided to act quickly.
to have the same opinion
We all agree on the plan.
to say sorry
You should apologise for your rude behaviour.
express regret for something one has done wrong
You should apologize for your rude behavior.
seem, become visible
He didn't appear to be happy.
to make a formal request; to put something on a surface
You should apply for the job by Friday.
to express disagreement
They often argue about politics.
to organize or put in order
Can you arrange the chairs for the meeting?
to be present at an event, meeting, or institution
She decided to attend the university in London.
to look after children while their parents are out
My older sister often babysits for our neighbors.
to cook (food) by dry heat, especially in an oven
She loves to bake cakes for her friends.
wash oneself
She likes to bathe in warm water.
to defeat
Our team can beat them.
to act or conduct oneself in a specified way
The children behaved well at the party.
accept as true
I believe in you.
be a part of, be owned by
This book belongs on the top shelf.
bend
Don't bend the book pages.
shut and open the eyes quickly
She didn't even blink when she heard the surprising news.
(of wind) move creating an air current
The wind began to blow strongly.
reach or cause to reach the temperature at which it bubbles and turns to vapor
Please boil some water for the tea.
to take and use (something belonging to someone else) with the intention of returning it
Can I borrow your pen?
to construct
They plan to build a new house.
to be on fire; to destroy by fire
Be careful not to burn yourself.
call off, annul
They had to cancel the flight due to bad weather.
feel concern or interest
I care about my family.
to intercept and hold something that has been thrown
He tried to catch the ball.
to mark an occasion with festivities
We will celebrate her birthday next weekend.
make or become different
The weather can change quickly.
chase
The dog loves to chase squirrels in the park.
to talk in a friendly, informal way
We had a nice chat over coffee.
to verify
Please check your answers carefully.
to break food with your teeth
Remember to chew your food slowly.
to select from a number of alternatives
You can choose any book you like.
cut (something) into small pieces
Please chop the vegetables for the soup.
strike the palms of (one's hands) together repeatedly
The audience began to clap loudly after the performance.
to press a button on a computer mouse or other device.
Click here to open the link.
ascend, go up
We decided to climb the mountain.
to gather together
He likes to collect stamps from different countries.
to add color to
She likes to colour her hair.
to tidy hair with a comb
She combs her hair every morning.
to share or exchange information, news, or ideas
It's important to communicate clearly.
express dissatisfaction
She always complains about the weather.
to join together
Can you connect these two wires?
continue
We will continue our discussion tomorrow.
to expel air from the lungs with a sudden sharp sound
He started to cough loudly.
decide
I need to decide what to do next.
to make something look more attractive
We decided to decorate the living room.
remove, erase
Please delete the old files from your computer.
rely on, be contingent on
Our picnic depends on the weather.
to make a call by pressing numbers on a phone
Please dial the emergency number if you need help.
to stop living
Many plants die in winter.
excavate, make a hole
The dog loves to dig holes in the garden.
to have a different opinion
I disagree with your point of view.
to vanish
The magician made the rabbit disappear.
to find unexpectedly or during a search
They hope to discover a cure for the disease.
to plunge head first into water
He learned to dive into the swimming pool.
to separate into parts or shares
We need to divide the cake into equal slices.
to let or make something fall
Be careful not to drop the glass.
to obtain money in return for labor or services
He works hard to earn a living.
to cause strong feelings of enthusiasm and eagerness
The news excited everyone.
to think that something will happen
I expect him to arrive soon.
make clear
Can you explain this to me.
to be unsuccessful in achieving one's goal.
He failed the exam.
to attach or close securely
Please fasten your seatbelt before the plane takes off.
to give food to
Don't forget to feed the dog.
Go for and then bring back (something).
Can you fetch me a glass of water?
make full
Please fill the bottle with water.
to repair, to make ready
Can you fix my broken phone?
rest on water or air
A boat can float on water.
to move through the air
Birds can fly.
To bend something over on itself so that part of it covers another part.
Please fold your clothes neatly.
follow
Please follow me.
past tense of find; establish or originate
I found my keys under the couch.
turn into ice or be turned into ice
The water will freeze if the temperature drops below zero.
wrinkle forehead in displeasure
He began to frown when he heard the bad news.
to cook in hot fat or oil
She decided to fry some eggs for breakfast.
to come together; to collect
The family will gather for dinner tonight.
to greet
She always greets me with a smile.
to grow
Plants grow towards the sunlight.
suspend or be suspended
Please hang your coat on the hook.
to put or keep out of sight
The children love to hide and seek.
to jump on one foot
The rabbit hopped across the garden.
to embrace
She gave her friend a warm hug.
to chase and kill animals for food or sport
They went out to hunt deer.
to move or act quickly
We need to hurry or we'll miss the train.
to hurt
Did you hurt your leg?
to form a mental image or concept of
Can you imagine a world without internet?
to present someone or something
Let me introduce you to my colleague.
to ask someone to come somewhere or do something
We invited them to our party.
have or cause an uncomfortable sensation on the skin that provokes a desire to scratch

run at a steady, gentle pace
I like to jog in the park every morning.
to connect or combine
Would you like to join us for dinner?
to retain; to continue
Please keep the change.
to strike with the foot
He tried to kick the ball into the net.
to touch with the lips as a sign of love, greeting, or respect
She leaned in to kiss her child goodnight.
to put something down gently
Please lay the book on the table.
to give something temporarily
Can you lend me your pen for a moment?
raise to a higher position or level
Can you help me lift this box?
fasten or secure with a lock
Please lock the door when you leave.
to join in marriage
They plan to marry next spring.
reduce (food) to a soft pulpy mass by crushing it
Please mash the potatoes for dinner.
intend to convey
What does this word mean?
to make or become liquefied by heat
The ice cream will melt quickly in the sun.
miss
I miss my family when I travel.
to fail to understand correctly
I think you misunderstand what I'm trying to say.
combine
Mix the ingredients well before baking.
cut grass
I need to mow the lawn this weekend.
to follow rules or commands
Dogs are trained to obey commands.
to cover a surface with paint
We decided to paint the living room blue.
to move or cause to move in a specified direction
Please pass the salt.
to remove the outer covering or skin from (a fruit or vegetable)
Can you peel the potatoes for dinner?
to flow or cause to flow in a steady stream
She poured water into the glass.
to perform an activity or exercise regularly
You need to practise every day to improve your skills.
to like better than another
I prefer coffee to tea.
to make (something) ready for use or consideration
We need to prepare for the exam.
to behave as if something is true when you know it is not
The children love to pretend they are superheroes.
to produce text or images on paper
Please print this document for me.
make or manufacture from components or raw materials
This factory produces cars.
(of a cat) make a low, continuous vibratory sound
The cat purred contentedly on her lap.
to arrive at; to extend to
Can you reach the book on the top shelf?
to become aware of something.
I didn't realize how late it was.
to be given, presented with, or paid (something)
I received a letter yesterday.
identify; know again
I didn't recognize you at first.
to convert waste into reusable material
Don't forget to recycle your plastic bottles.
Fill again
Could you please refill my coffee cup?
to heat again
Please reheat the leftovers in the microwave.
to make or become less tense or anxious
After work, I like to relax with a book.
make someone remember
Please remind me to call him.
to give a new name to
They decided to rename the street.
to pay for the use of something
We decided to rent a car for our vacation.
to say or do again
Could you please repeat that?
say something in response to something someone has said

to say or do something as a reaction to something that has been said or done
Please respond to my email as soon as possible.
to start again
If the computer freezes, you should restart it.
to move from a lower to a higher position
The sun will rise at 6 AM tomorrow.
cook (food, especially meat) by prolonged exposure to heat in an oven or over a fire
We decided to roast a chicken for dinner.
to move by turning over and over
The ball started to roll down the hill.
to move your hand over a surface
She rubbed her eyes sleepily.
to travel on water in a ship or boat
We sailed across the lake.
rescue, store
I need to save money.
to frighten
Don't scare the cat.
yell loudly
She began to scream when she saw the spider.
seem
It seems like a good idea.
to exchange for money
They want to sell their old car.
perform duties or services for; present food or drink
He served in the army for ten years.
to join, fasten, or repair by making stitches with a needle and thread or a sewing machine
My grandmother taught me how to sew a button.
to move quickly to and fro or up and down
He shook his head in disagreement.
remove hair with a razor
He needs to shave his beard.
emit light; be bright or luminous
The sun began to shine brightly.
(of a person or animal) tremble slightly and uncontrollably as a result of being cold, frightened, or excited
She began to shiver from the cold.
shut
Please shut the door quietly.
to go down below the surface of water
The ship began to sink after hitting the iceberg.
to glide on skates
They love to skate on the ice rink.
to move over snow on skis
We plan to ski in the mountains this winter.
to jump lightly; to miss or omit
Let's skip the next chapter and go straight to the end.
to expel air from the nose and mouth suddenly
He tried to suppress a sneeze during the quiet lecture.
make a snorting or grunting sound while asleep
He tends to snore loudly when he sleeps on his back.
find an answer to
Can you help me solve this puzzle?
to take (another person's property) without permission or legal right
Someone tried to steal my bike.
mix with a spoon
Please stir the soup slowly.
to take away
If you subtract 5 from 10, you get 5.
achieve a goal; follow in order
She worked hard to succeed in her career.
cause food or drink to pass down the throat
He found it hard to swallow the large pill.
to clean with a broom
Please sweep the floor.
to propel something through the air with a rapid movement of the arm and hand.
Throw the ball to me.
fasten or secure with string or rope
Can you help me tie my shoelaces?
become weary
Running a marathon will tire you out.
to convert from one language to another
Can you translate this sentence into Spanish?
to take off one's clothes
She quickly undressed and got into bed.
to open (something locked) by means of a key or other device

to remove contents from a container

disconnect (an electrical device) by pulling out its plug

to undo or unfasten (something tied or knotted)

to stop sleeping
I usually wake up at 7 AM.
to inform someone of a possible danger

find out how heavy something is
We need to weigh the luggage before going to the airport.
to clean or dry (something) by rubbing its surface with a cloth or one's hand

to feel or cause to feel anxious or troubled
Don't worry about the exam.
to cover something completely

to open one's mouth wide and inhale deeply due to tiredness or boredom

to shout loudly
Don't yell at me, I can hear you perfectly.
